what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon that draws a rounded square outline, representing a maximize-style control.
observations: Uses a single rect element as the visible shape.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke-width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The rectangle starts at x=4 and y=4, spans 16 by 16, and has rx=1.
standouts: The visible mark is a square outline rather than arrows or corner marks.
conventions: Follows common 24x24 stroked icon SVG conventions.
Uses inline SVG attributes rather than CSS classes.
Keeps the icon monochrome and themeable through currentColor.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility.
The maximize meaning depends on filename or UI context because the graphic itself is only a square outline.
notes: A minimal themeable SVG icon for a maximize control, drawn as a rounded square outline.
questions: Is this intended to be decorative only, or should accessibility metadata be supplied by the consuming component?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a maximize icon, and the file renders a square-outline control consistent with that purpose at a minimal visual level.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a lightweight, reusable maximize icon that can inherit color from surrounding UI text or controls.
contribution: Contributes a compact, themeable maximize-control glyph defined with a single stroked rectangle and standard 24x24 icon sizing.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 SVG maximize icon made from one currentColor stroked rounded rectangle with no fill.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a maximize action in a reusable, monochrome design-system icon asset.
